incidents.title: Cargo Contamination - Residue from Previous Cargo
incidents.description:
During pre-loading survey at Aliaga for the current voyage (Clinker, 1484 MT for Eagle Bulk Shipping Inc.), the independent surveyor
(appointed by Charterers) rejected Hold No. 2 and Hold No. 4 due to traces of previous cargo (Iron Ore) found under the hopper tank
plating and behind frames. Vessel had discharged Iron Ore at Vizag 12 days earlier and holds were swept and washed by crew.
incidents.root_cause:
Insufficient cleaning of hold bilge areas and behind frames after previous cargo. Standard crew cleaning procedure did not adequately
address residue accumulation in hard-to-reach areas for the specific previous cargo type.

incidents.title: Crane Wire Rope Parted During Discharge
incidents.description:
On 15 May 2024 at approximately 02:45, during discharge operations at Kohsichang, the wire rope of Deck Crane No. 3 (30MT SWL,
manufactured by NOV/MacGREGOR) parted while lifting a cargo grab loaded with approximately 12 MT of Coal (Thermal). The grab
fell approximately 4 meters back into the hold. No personnel were in the hold at the time of the incident.
incidents.root_cause:
Wire rope failure due to fatigue and accumulated wear. Inspection of the parted end showed multiple broken wires in the outer strands
concentrated at the section running over the crane head sheave. Wire installed 18 months ago with approximately 2,400 operating
hours. Discard criteria per manufacturer: 6 broken wires in one lay length - inspection prior to this voyage noted 4 broken wires.

incidents.title: Cargo Wetting - Hold No. 3
incidents.description:
During discharge operations at Qingdao, stevedores reported wet cargo in upper stow of Hold No. 3. Approximately 1403 MT of
Manganese Ore found damp/wet along portside shell plating. Investigation revealed condensation drip from hatch coaming gutters,
possibly due to blocked scupper drainage channel. Weather during laden passage included heavy tropical rain (cumulative 180mm
over 3 days).
incidents.root_cause:
Blocked scupper drainage on Hold 3 hatch coaming allowed rainwater accumulation during tropical passage. Gasket seal found intact
but water ingress from overflow of drain channel.

incidents.title: Main Engine Turbocharger Failure
incidents.description:
At approximately 01:45 on 30 March 2026, while vessel was in laden condition en route from New Orleans to Qingdao (position: Lat
21.2N Lon 109.9E), the Main Engine No.1 Turbocharger (MAN TCR-18) developed severe vibration followed by bearing seizure. ME
output immediately reduced from 85% MCR to 45% MCR on remaining T/C. Vessel speed reduced from 13.5 knots to approximately
8.5 knots.
incidents.root_cause:
Failure of turbocharger thrust bearing due to lube oil contamination. Oil sample analysis (later confirmed) showed elevated iron
particles indicating progressive wear. Last T/C overhaul 14,500 running hours ago (recommended interval: 16,000 hours) - bearing
was approaching end of life.

incidents.title: Close Quarters Situation - Traffic Separation Scheme
incidents.description:
On 02 October 2025 at approximately 23:30, while vessel was transiting Mozambique Channel, a close quarters situation developed
with a container vessel (approx. 8000 TEU) crossing from port to starboard at a CPA of approximately 0.3 nautical miles. The target
vessel appeared to be cutting across the TSS lanes without following Rule 10 of COLREGS.
incidents.root_cause:
Third-party vessel non-compliance with TSS regulations. Own vessel was maintaining proper lookout and following traffic separation
scheme correctly. The incident highlights the ongoing risk of non-compliant vessels in high-traffic areas.

incidents.title: Heavy Weather Damage - Deck Fittings
incidents.description:
Vessel encountered severe weather in Cape of Good Hope on 02 September 2025. Wind Force 10-11 (Beaufort), sea state 8-9,
significant wave height estimated 8-10 meters. Despite reducing speed and altering course to minimize vessel motions, several heavy
seas broke over the forecastle and main deck. Vessel experienced heavy rolling (maximum 32 degrees) and pitching.
incidents.root_cause:
Exceptional weather event exceeding forecast predictions. Vessel's routing service (DTN/StormGeo) had predicted maximum BF 8.
Actual conditions significantly worse due to rapidly developing secondary low-pressure system.
